original_hypothesis.title: ProxyOracle can be set to a non-contract oracleImplementation, causing system-wide oracle DoS where used
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ProxyOracle.changeOracleImplementation does not validate that `newOracle` has code or implements the expected interface. If set to an EOA or incompatible contract, all calls (get/peek/peekSpot/decimals) will revert, potentially bricking any Cauldron or subsystem relying on that oracle for exchange rates and solvency checks.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a sharp-edge admin operation with high blast radius where ProxyOracle is used. It is a code-level missing validation but exploitation requires privileged access (owner).
deployment_assumptions: ProxyOracle is used as oracle in one or more Cauldrons or critical pricing paths
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence in provided scripts that ProxyOracle is used or configured; hypothesis applies if integrated post-deploy.

proof_of_signal.pattern: changeOracleImplementation(): sets oracleImplementation without code/interface validation; downstream functions blindly call oracleImplementation.*

preconditions: ProxyOracle owner sets oracleImplementation incorrectly (EOA or incompatible oracle)
A dependent contract calls ProxyOracle.get/peek/peekSpot during critical flow (borrow/liquidate/solvency check)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Oracle indirection lacks sanity checks; misconfiguration leads to revert cascades.

operational_questions: Is ProxyOracle used anywhere in deployed Cauldrons (oracle address points to ProxyOracle)?
Are there runbooks/tests ensuring newOracle is a contract and returns sane values?
validation_requirements: Static scan of deployments for ProxyOracle usage
Simulate setting oracleImplementation to EOA and observe failure modes in Cauldron.updateExchangeRate/accrue flows

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If privileged: set oracleImplementation to invalid address

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium-High: core lending actions can halt if oracle calls revert
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (requires corrective owner tx; may be blocked if other dependencies fail)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: OracleImplementationChange events; sudden oracle call reverts
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Spike in failed borrows/liquidations due to oracle failure

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
